page: Microsoft Azure F16s_v2 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the FSv2 server family. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz, this instance features 16 vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It includes 131 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark results show balanced performance across compute and memory operations. The instance achieved a stress-ng single-core score of 1445.09 and a multi-core score of 14872.23. In memory bandwidth testing, it reached 831.46 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 81.36 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. For specialized workloads, the server demonstrated a Geekbench compiling software score of 12769.0, a ray tracing score of 11925.0, and a Redis SET throughput of 13.89 million operations per second.

With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the F16s_v2 offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The dedicated CPU allocation and AVX-512 support ensure predictable execution times. This server is well-suited for tasks such as software compilation, web serving, batch processing, and lightweight machine learning inference, as evidenced by its prompt processing speed of 745.96 tokens per second on a small 135M parameter model.
description: Microsoft Azure F16s_v2 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the FSv2 family, featuring 16 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz, this x86_64 instance provides a dedicated CPU allocation with 8 physical cores and hyper-threading. It includes 131 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks indicate strong compute capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 14872.23, a Geekbench software compilation score of 12769.0, and a Redis SET throughput of 13.89 million operations per second. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this instance a cost-efficient choice for compute-intensive workloads that do not require high memory density. It is highly suitable for software compilation, web serving, batch processing, and small-scale model inference.

tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring sixteen dedicated vCPUs and a low memory-to-core ratio for high-throughput processing tasks.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per vCPU
